summary: Cipher backend: 13/13 pytest tests PASSED against public URL. Covered health, register/login/me/logout, duplicate email, unauthorized access, 2FA (setup+verify+login flow using pyotp), sessions, user search (excludes self), full server→channel→message flow with sender hydration, server join via invite, E2EE DM send/get/list-conversations (two users), file upload+download round-trip, and unauthorized endpoint enforcement.

minor.issue: CORS_ORIGINS='*' with allow_credentials falls back to False (per server.py logic). Cookie-based auth from a browser on a different origin would fail. Current preview uses same-origin via ingress so Bearer tokens work; but for production, set CORS_ORIGINS to explicit comma-separated origins.
log_session called with user['id'] only if user exists; unknown-email failed logins are logged with 'unknown'. Minor — consider storing attempted email for rate-limit/lockout.
No brute-force lockout / rate limiting on /api/auth/login (playbook recommends lockout after 5 fails). login_attempts collection is mentioned in context but unused in server.py.
No size limit or MIME allowlist — reads entire file into memory. Acceptable for MVP, but add streaming + 25MB cap for prod.
Uses sync file iterator inside async handler (yield from f). Works, but could block event loop for large files.

action_items: OPTIONAL: tighten CORS_ORIGINS to explicit frontend URL for production & keep allow_credentials=True
OPTIONAL: add brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (5 fails → temporary lock) using the unused login_attempts collection
OPTIONAL: add file size cap + streaming upload to /api/files/upload
critical_code_review_comments: bcrypt hashing format verified ($2b$) via round-trip login success.
JWT uses HS256 with 8h access + 7d refresh; both cookie and Bearer auth supported correctly.
Mongo _id properly excluded across queries (projection {'_id':0}) and popped after insert_one.
2FA: pending secret stored separately and only promoted to two_factor_secret after verify — good.
DM E2EE: stores both sender_ciphertext & recipient_ciphertext keyed by conversation_id=sorted(uid1,uid2) — correct for symmetric retrieval.
Default 'general' channel auto-created on server create — verified.
server.py is 594 lines — nearing the 700-line threshold. Consider splitting into routers/ (auth, servers, dms, files) soon.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; migrate to lifespan context manager.
Admin seed uses placeholder PENDING keys — documented in test_credentials.md; fresh users required for E2EE tests (handled in test suite).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@cipher.io / Admin@Cipher2026. Fresh users auto-created per test via /api/auth/register with random TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Each test registers its own TEST_* users. No cleanup implemented (acceptable; uses unique uuids).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core backend flows validated via /app/backend/tests/test_cipher_backend.py (pytest). Run: `pytest backend/tests/test_cipher_backend.py -v`. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var (falls back to public preview URL). 2FA test uses pyotp to generate real TOTP. If frontend testing is requested next, verify that the client generates RSA-OAEP 2048 keys in-browser (Web Crypto) and sends encrypted_private_key+key_salt in register payload.
